'''Steve Blakeley''' (born 17 October 1972) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] (two spells), and [[Warrington Wolves]], as a {{rlp|so}}, or {{rlp|sh}}.<ref name="Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order at wigan.rlfans.com">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|title=Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order at wigan.rlfans.com|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327102545/http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Career==
===Wigan===
Blakeley started his career at [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] where he made eight appearances as an [[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]. He made his début for Wigan against [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[1990–91 Rugby League Premiership]] first-round match during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Sunday 21 April 1991, scoring his first try for Wigan in a 26–31 defeat. He scored his last try for Wigan in the 31-14 victory over [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] in the [[1991–92 League Cup (rugby league)|1991–92 Regal Trophy]] preliminary-round during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Tuesday 29 October 1991, and he played his last match for Wigan in the 36-16 victory over [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Hilton Park (stadium)|Hilton Park]], [[Leigh, Greater Manchester|Leigh]] on Sunday 4 October 1992.<ref name="Statistics at wigan.rlfans.com">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=181|title=Statistics at wigan.rlfans.com|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref> 

===Salford===
In 1992, he was transferred from Wigan to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] for £28,000.<ref name="woodwardstyles">{{cite web|last=Woodward-Styles|first=Louise|title=Mr Salford: A Tribute to John Wilkinson|url=http://www.salfordonline.com/sport_page/39838-mr_salford:_a_tribute_to_john_wilkinson.html|publisher=SalfordOnline|accessdate=22 May 2014|date=3 December 2012|url-status=bot: unknown|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140523011120/http://www.salfordonline.com/sport_page/39838-mr_salford:_a_tribute_to_john_wilkinson.html|archivedate=23 May 2014}}</ref> He was transferred from the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] to the [[Warrington Wolves]] at the end of 1999's [[Super League IV]]. He made his début for Warrington Wolves on Sunday 13 February 2000, and he played his last match for Warrington Wolves on Monday 1 May 2000. He played 10 games for the Warrington Wolves, but returned to Salford City Reds in May 2000 after becoming frustrated at his lack of playing time.<ref>{{cite news|last=Burke|first=David|title=Rugby League: Steve Turns Red Again|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Rugby+League%3A+STEVE+TURNS+RED+AGAIN.-a061901292|newspaper=The Mirror|date=4 May 2000|location=London}}</ref> He then remained with Salford City Reds until his retirement in 2003. He holds Salford's "Most goals in a game" record with 14-goals scored against [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] on Sunday 23 March 2003. He is also Salford's third highest point scorer behind [[David Watkins (rugby)|David Watkins]] and [[Gus Risman]].<ref name="woodwardstyles" />

===International honours===
Blakeley won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Salford Reds in 1996 against France, and Wales ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and while at Salford City Reds in 1999 against France ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]).

==Physiotherapist==
After retiring from playing he served a number of clubs as a physiotherapist including [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Association football|football]] teams [[Manchester City F.C.|Manchester City]] and [[Bolton Wanderers F.C.|Bolton Wanderers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Blakeley to lead Giants' medical team |url=https://www.totalrl.com/blakeley-to-lead-giants-medical-team/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=15 July 2023 |date=15 July 2019}}</ref><ref>https://www.bwfc.co.uk/teams/club-staff/first-team-staff/steve-blakeley/, Bolton Wanderers profile</ref>
